raw.objective: “The NONMEM approach was used to evaluate the influence on patients of weight, race, gender, age, body surface area, smoking status, and mild-to-moderate alcohol consumption on Vm and Km”
raw.covariates: Tested: WT, RACE, SEX, AGE, BSA, smoking status, alcohol consumption on Vm and Km Included: WT, RACE, AGE, smoking status on Vm
raw.observations: Much unexplained variability remained after inclusion of the aforementioned covariates. Some bias can be seen resulting in underprediction of the higher concentrations, which may lead to underprediction of side-effects.
raw.structure: 1 compartment model with parallel michaelis menten and linear elimination
raw.equations: Vm = (theta1*WT+theta3)*(theta4**RACE)*(theta5**SMK)*(theta7**ELD)*exp(eta1) Km = theta2*exp(eta2) CL = theta6*exp(eta3) Css = -(1/2)*(((Vm/CL)+Km-(DD/CL))-sqrt((((Vm/CL)+Km-(DD/CL))**2)+((4*DD*Km)/CL))) RACE is race (0 if black; 1 if colored) SMK is smoker (0 if not smoking, 1 if smoking) AGE is age (0 if younger than 65 years, 1 if older)
raw.parameter_values: theta1=3.86 theta2=7.45 theta3=112 theta4=1.10 theta5=1.16 theta6=2.31 theta7=0.88 omega1=0.0156 omega2=0.461 omega3=0.214

structural_model.description: 1 compartment model with parallel michaelis menten and linear elimination

omega.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending
Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending
Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.objective: “The NONMEM approach was used to evaluate the influence on patients of weight, race, gender, age, body surface area, smoking status, and mild-to-moderate alcohol consumption on Vm and Km”

tested: alcohol consumption on Vm and Km
covariates.raw: Tested: WT, RACE, SEX, AGE, BSA, smoking status, alcohol consumption on Vm and Km Included: WT, RACE, AGE, smoking status on Vm

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
